items.title: New York Times says OpenAI hid evidence in ChatGPT copyright trial

items.title: Google will now disclose which ads are made with AI
items.summary: While Google prohibits misleading and deceptive ads, an ad can still leverage AI to create some type of synthetic or digitally altered content. Until now, that's something Google only required election ads to disclose.

items.summary: The company is using the cash to open an office in the Bay Area and compete for talent there, "strengthening its position at the heart of the world's leading AI ecosystem."

items.title: How to stop Meta’s AI image generator from using your Instagram photos
items.summary: Muse Image allows users to generate AI images using photos from public Instagram accounts. As long as a person's profile is public, another user can tag that account and use their images as part of an AI-generated creation.

items.title: Amazon knocks MSI’s RTX 5060 Katana 15 HX gaming laptop below Prime Day low at $1,134
items.summary: We just spotted a deal at Amazon that drops the MSI Katana 15 HX gaming laptop down to $1,133.67 shipped. This configuration has typically been sitting above the $1,500 mark at Amazon, meaning today’s deal knocks at least $366 off its usual going rate and delivers nearly 25% in savings. It dropped to $1,140 during the recent Prime Day sale, but today’s offer takes another $6 off that price to land at the best price we have tracked for this RTX 5060 gaming laptop so far. items.title: Nvidia is a victim of the compute marketplace it created
items.summary: Having proven how valuable compute can be, the company finds itself at the center of a market everyone wants to be in — while simpler technologies and less interesting companies get rich on the sidelines.

items.title: Alienware just unleashed its 30th anniversary gaming monitor lineup with 240Hz models from $300
items.summary: Alienware is celebrating 30 years of gaming hardware with a fresh trio of new gaming monitors, and these new models are now up for grabs for your battlestation. The new range includes the flagship 34-inch AW3426DW QD-OLED model at $799.99 shipped, along with two more affordable 240Hz options – the 34-inch AW3426DWM at $399.99 shipped and the 32-inch AW3226DM at $299.99 shipped. The brand is essentially bringing its premium ultrawide and high-refresh gaming experience to a wider price range with these new displays, so you don’t necessarily have to jump all the way to a flagship monitor to get a serious upgrade.
